title: Trema molle
intro: Trema molle, also known as Malabar button or monkey face, is a small, deciduous tree native to tropical Asia and Australia, belonging to the family Salicaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its antipyretic and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Trema molle include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Trema molle is commonly prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Trema molle belongs to the family Salicaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Trema. The genus Trema is a part of the order Sapindales. Within this order, the Salicaceae family is a part of the larger group known as the Rosidae. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships between Trema molle and other plants.
uses: Trema molle has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In some regions, the plant is used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and bark are often used in traditional medicine to make teas and infusions. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses for Trema molle.

side_effects: Trema molle is a plant with various medicinal properties, but its use can have potential side effects.
